title: Indentation and Blocks
summary: Understand why indentation controls structure in Python.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding Indentation
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In Python, indentation is not just for readability; it defines the structure of the code. Blocks of code are defined by their indentation level.

sketchBlocks.title: Blocks and Control Flow
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: Control flow statements like if, elif, and else rely on indentation to determine which statements belong to which block.

quizDraft.title: What does indentation control in Python?
quizDraft.prompt: Choose the correct statement about indentation.
options: It controls the execution speed of the program.
It defines the structure of code blocks.
It is optional and can be ignored.
It is used only for comments.
quizDraft.hint: Think about how Python interprets code structure.

help.concept: Indentation in Python defines the grouping of statements. It is essential for the correct execution of control flow.
help.hint_1: Indentation is not just for aesthetics; it has functional importance.
help.hint_2: Consider how blocks of code are formed in other programming languages.

quizDraft.title: Which of the following statements about blocks in Python are true?
options: Each block must start with a new indentation level.
Blocks can be nested within other blocks.
Indentation can be mixed with tabs and spaces.
quizDraft.hint: Focus on the main concept being tested and eliminate choices that describe something different.

help.concept: Choose the option that matches the core idea described in the question without relying on repeated wording from the answer choices.
help.hint_1: Rule out options that describe a different role, behavior, or concept than the question is testing.
help.hint_2: Pick the option that best matches the concept, not the one that simply repeats familiar wording.

quizDraft.prompt: In Python, indentation is used to define a ______.
quizDraft.template: In Python, indentation is used to define a {blank}.
